body:
Phase 3B upgrade done, carefully: SLS(78) -> SLS(79)/9.93.

WARRANT#75 (real PBO): a simplified, single-split PBO-style estimate -- explicitly NOT the full Bailey & Lopez de Prado CSCV method, which would need a much bigger redesign. Sampled every 500th draw (~100 of the 50,000) inside Stage 6's global weight search, reusing values the loop already computes for its own acceptance check -- one array write on top of work already done, zero new bar-scanning added. The actual statistic (do in-sample-best candidates perform below-median out-of-sample -- the overfitting signature) is computed once per regime, after the loop, on at most 100 numbers.

Before shipping I diffed the entire change against the pre-pass build: only the version banner changed textually, everything else is pure addition. The loop's Sleep(1) cadence (the thing that had to be patched once before, after a real Windows-killed-the-terminal incident) and its accept/reject logic are byte-for-byte untouched. Compiled 0 errors; two new warnings the local arrays triggered got cleaned up with ArrayInitialize so the build matches the exact prior baseline (1 pre-existing warning, nothing new).

This now feeds ForgeScorecard.pbo (worst-of-9-regimes) with a real number for the first time, replacing the permanent reject-all 1.0 default -- meaning the ratchet's PBO gate can actually pass something for the first time, if the numbers support it. Still unvalidated against a real run, same as everything else. Attaching final SLS(79) mq4+ex4 and the updated forge_ledger.json (59 passes).
